scope: Core mathematical notation for reading, translation, and paper-level explanation. This is not a complete inventory of all mathematical symbols.

reading_policy.direct: Name of the visible symbol or notation.
reading_policy.read_aloud.default_zh: Default Chinese oral reading when context is ordinary mathematical prose.
reading_policy.read_aloud.default_en: Default English oral reading when context is ordinary mathematical prose.
reading_policy.read_aloud.structural_en: More explicit structural/accessibility-style reading; useful for screen readers, speech-to-LaTeX, or ambiguity-sensitive contexts.
reading_policy.semantic_default: Default semantic interpretation, usually contextual rather than absolute.

candidates.when: Function declaration/type signature: f: X → Y.
State transition, rewriting, dynamics: s_t → s_{t+1}.

candidates.zh: 从 A 到 B 的箭头 / 态射
candidates.en: an arrow/morphism from A to B
candidates.when: Category theory or graph-like abstract arrows.
Function mapping when a compact oral reading is needed.
Algorithm, data construction, or expression-to-dataset transformation.
Unclear semantics; read the visible arrow directly.

symbols.notes: Do not use 'maps to' as the default for f: X → Y. In that context, the standard oral reading is usually 'f from X to Y'.

candidates.when: Rule of a function: x ↦ f(x).
Explicit mapsto notation or element image under a function.

symbols.notes: Use → for domain/codomain, and ↦ for the image of an element under a rule.

candidates.when: Identity over a domain, often stated in prose rather than by symbol alone.
Only when surrounding text explicitly says 'let', 'define', or 'denote'.

symbols.notes: Plain '=' should not default to 'is defined as'. Prefer :=, \coloneqq, or \triangleq for explicit definitions.

candidates.when: Definitions, notation introduction, assignment-like mathematical definitions.

candidates.zh: 冒号等号 / 定义等号

symbols.notes: This should be separated from ordinary equality.

read_aloud.default_zh: x 帽 / x 的估计量

candidates.when: Statistics, regression, approximation, learned model output, or estimator context.
Physics/vector notation, e.g. x-hat as a unit vector.

symbols.notes: Do not force 'estimated value' outside statistics/ML context.

candidates.when: General indexed variable; use as a compact oral connector before the index.
Notation such as f_x, if defined as derivative.

symbols.notes: Use 'subscript' for the symbol category; 'sub' is the usual oral abbreviation.

read_aloud.default_en: x to the n-th / x to the power n

candidates.when: Tensor, layer, or sample notation if defined locally.

symbols.notes: Do not force every superscript to be exponentiation.

candidates.when: Piecewise functions with a left brace.

symbols.notes: Braces are delimiters; 'set' is only one semantic use.

read_aloud.default_zh: x_i 和 y_i 组成的有序对
read_aloud.default_en: ordered pair x sub i, y sub i / tuple
read_aloud.structural_en: open parenthesis x sub i comma y sub i close parenthesis

symbols.notes: This entry is useful, but it should be subordinate to the more general parentheses entry.

read_aloud.structural_en: fraction with numerator a and denominator b

candidates.when: Probability notation may use p(x|y), not usually \frac.

symbols.notes: For nested fractions, structural reading may be safer than ordinary oral reading.

read_aloud.default_zh: 根号 x / x 的平方根

candidates.when: Real/nonnegative or complex principal branch context.

symbols.notes: In formal analysis, clarify principal branch if complex values are involved.

candidates.when: Set theory or probability, if local notation defines it.

symbols.notes: Default semantic meaning should be contextual, not always mean.

candidates.zh: 近似量 / 扰动量 / 变换变量
candidates.when: Approximation, transformed variable, normalized variable, perturbed quantity.
Signal processing or ML notation if defined locally.

symbols.notes: Do not confuse accent \tilde{x} with relation x \sim D.

candidates.when: Informal proof step, if not used as a formal connective.

symbols.notes: Avoid calling it only 'double arrow' in Chinese because that can be confused with bidirectional arrows.
This is a high-priority missing symbol if ⇒ is included.

candidates.when: Function call: f(x), read 'f of x'.

symbols.notes: Do not default every parenthesized comma expression to tuple without checking context.

candidates.when: Programming or discrete math notation: A[i].

symbols.notes: Important for CS/ML papers.

read_aloud.default_zh: f 从 X 到 Y

candidates.zh: 函数定义域和值域分隔

symbols.notes: Colon is essential for correctly reading f: X → Y.

read_aloud.default_en: is in / is an element of
is not in / is not an element of
is a subset of / is contained in

candidates.zh: 子集 / 真子集，依作者约定
candidates.en: subset / proper subset depending on convention

symbols.notes: Use \subseteq for unambiguous 'subset or equal'. Some authors use ⊂ inconsistently.

read_aloud.default_zh: 包含于或等于 / 是……的子集
read_aloud.default_en: is a subset of or equal to

candidates.en: is a subset of or equal to

read_aloud.default_zh: A 减去 B / A 与 B 的差集
read_aloud.default_en: A set minus B / A minus B

read_aloud.default_zh: 从 i 等于 1 到 n 对 a_i 求和
read_aloud.default_en: the sum from i equals one to n of a sub i
read_aloud.structural_en: upper sigma with subscript i equals one and superscript n

read_aloud.default_zh: 从 i 等于 1 到 n 对 a_i 连乘
read_aloud.default_en: the product from i equals one to n of a sub i
read_aloud.structural_en: upper pi with subscript i equals one and superscript n

read_aloud.default_zh: 当 x 趋向于 0 时 f(x) 的极限
read_aloud.default_en: the limit as x approaches zero of f of x
read_aloud.structural_en: limit with subscript x approaches zero

candidates.when: Limit, extended real number, cardinality context.

read_aloud.default_zh: f 对 x 的偏导数
read_aloud.default_en: the partial derivative of f with respect to x

direct.zh: nabla 符号 / 倒三角算子

read_aloud.default_zh: 从 a 到 b 对 f(x) 关于 x 积分
read_aloud.default_en: the integral from a to b of f of x d x
read_aloud.structural_en: integral with lower limit a and upper limit b

symbols.notes: This is a canonical ambiguity; semantic reading needs context.

read_aloud.structural_en: double vertical bar x double vertical bar

read_aloud.default_en: does not equal / is not equal to

symbols.notes: Important omission for any basic symbol table.

candidates.when: Some fields use \simeq for similarity or approximate isomorphism.

symbols.notes: Do not collapse ≈, ∼, ≃, ≅ without domain-specific rules.

read_aloud.default_en: is equivalent to / is identically equal to

candidates.when: Number theory: a ≡ b mod n.

symbols.notes: The meaning is strongly field-dependent.

read_aloud.default_en: is less than or equal to

candidates.en: is less than or equal to

read_aloud.default_en: is greater than or equal to

candidates.en: is greater than or equal to

read_aloud.default_zh: 服从 / 相似于 / 渐近等价于
read_aloud.default_en: is distributed as / is similar to / is asymptotic to

candidates.en: is similar to / is equivalent under a relation

symbols.notes: This relation was missing and is distinct from \tilde{x}.

candidates.when: When T is a label, not transpose.

symbols.notes: Better represented as a separate symbol pattern, not only a candidate under generic superscript.

read_aloud.default_en: A inverse / the inverse of A

symbols.notes: For scalars, 'reciprocal' may be more precise than 'inverse'.

read_aloud.default_zh: f 撇 / f 的一阶导数

symbols.notes: Prime is frequently used and should be included.

candidates.when: When discussing typography rather than semantics.

symbols.notes: In ML papers, bold symbols often indicate vectors/matrices; this is convention, not guaranteed.
Missing if \sqrt{x} is included.

read_aloud.default_en: dot dot dot / and so on / up to
